items.question: It’s on a Friday — what should we know?
items.answer: Not much, happily — the whole celebration is the evening of Friday, June 18, 2027, start to finish. Come for the night and head home after, or stay over nearby; either works.

items.question: What time does the ceremony start?
items.answer: The ceremony begins at **6:00 pm** at The Ryland Inn. Doors open beforehand, so please aim to arrive a little early. The full run of the evening is on the [Schedule](schedule.html).

items.question: Where exactly is Whitehouse Station?
items.answer: It’s a small community in Hunterdon County, in the rolling western part of New Jersey, about an hour from Newark Airport. Directions, airports, the train, and drive times are all on the [Travel](travel.html) page.

items.question: What’s the dress code?
items.answer: **Garden formal** — suits and floor- or cocktail-length dresses; summer colors are very welcome.

items.question: Will it be indoors?
items.answer: Yes — the ceremony and the reception are both in the Grand Ballroom at The Ryland Inn, so you’re covered whatever the June weather decides to do.

items.question: Is there parking at the venue?
items.answer: Yes — there’s plenty of complimentary parking at The Ryland Inn, and you’re welcome to leave your car overnight and collect it the next day.

items.question: Can I bring a guest?
items.answer: Your invitation will name everyone we’ve saved a seat for, so it’ll say right on your RSVP. If you’re unsure, just ask us — we’re easy to reach and happy to help.

items.question: Are kids invited?
items.answer: We love the children in our lives dearly — and this is a grown-ups’ evening.

items.question: Where are you registered?
items.answer: We’ve put together a small registry and a honeymoon fund — it’s all on the [Registry](registry.html) page. Truly, though, your being there is the gift.

items.question: Is there a hotel block?
items.answer: Yes — we’re holding a room block at a nearby hotel, with a few other options close by. Details and booking links are on the [Travel](travel.html) page.

items.question: Is transportation provided?
items.answer: Yes — we’ll run a shuttle between the hotel block and the venue on the evening of the wedding, so you won’t need to drive. Times will be confirmed closer to the day.

items.question: How do I get there — do I need a car?
items.answer: Most guests fly into Newark and drive in, and a car is the easiest way around. If you’d rather take the train from the city, there’s a route on the [Travel](travel.html) page — and the shuttle covers the hop from the hotel to the venue.
